name: Duck with Black Bean Sauce and Tamarind Jus
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix the stuffing ingredients in the cavity of each duck.
2. Trim excess fat and bind the legs at the tail end.
3. Season with salt and pepper, then roast in a 325°F oven for 2 hours.
4. Pour off any grease when the ducks are golden brown.
5. Remove from the oven and cool to room temperature.
6. Halve the ducks along the backbone and debone.
7. Discard the stuffing and place the bones in 4 cups of water with an onion, some carrot, salt, and pepper. Simmer for about 2 hours. Reserve the duck stock.
8. Black bean sauce: Blanch beans in water until soft, then chop coarsely. Skin and puree the apple. Sweat shallots in port wine until translucent. Combine all ingredients with beans and pureed apple, simmering for about 15 minutes until flavors blend. Stir in butter and set aside.
9. Tamarind jus: Soak tamarind in water until soft, squeeze out seeds, strain, and puree pulp. Combine puree and duck stock, heating through. Stir in butter.
10. Place skin side down in a roasting pan in a 500°F oven for about 10 minutes (until skin is crispy). Pour black bean sauce over four plates and top with ducks skin side up. Drizzle tamarind jus over the ducks and serve with oriental vegetables.
